raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Consumer complaints have mounted over third-party websites charging excessive fees for DMV services.",
    "Many third-party websites closely resemble the DMV’s official site but charge hidden fees that can double or triple standard costs.",
    "An elderly customer seeking to renew their vehicle registration was charged $303 by a deceptive website, while the standard fee should have been $221.",
    "A consumer renewing two cars paid $956 after being hit with more than $150 in hidden fees, and only one registration was completed.",
    "Predatory websites rely on confusing seniors, families, and low-income Californians.",
    "Currently, California caps DMV service fees charged by authorized business partners, but no protections apply to third-party online providers.",
    "The proposed legislation would allow third-party websites to charge only a small, regulated convenience fee above DMV rates."
  ]
}
```
